case_title: UNITED GROWTH, LLC VS. SURVEY AMERICA, INC. et al

docket_entries.description: REQUEST TO ENTER DEFAULT FILED/DEFAULT ENTERED, DECLARATION OF NON-MILITARY STATUS (SET ASIDE PER ORDER OF JAN 28, 2011) AS TO DEFENDANT SURVEY AMERICA, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BROWN CONSULTING ENGINEERS MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS CONTINUED FROM SEP-03-2009 TO LAW AND MOTION AT SEP-17-2009, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, PER AGREEMENT OF PARTIES.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BROWN CONSULTING ENGINEERS MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. DEFENDANT DID NOT PURPOSEFULLY AVAIL ITSELF OF THE PRIVILEGE OF CONDUCTING ACTIVITIES IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT WAS HIRED BY AN INDIANA COMPANY, NEVER TRAVELED TO CALIFORNIA, DID NOT WORK IN CALIFORNIA, AND DID NOT EXPECT TO BE PAID IN CALIFORNIA. THE CASE INVOLVES LAND IN UTAH. IT WOULD NOT BE REASONABLE TO EXERCISE JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Sep-17-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BROWN CONSULTING ENGINEERS Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Appearances: Thomas T. McCormick, Esq. Attorney at Law, (925) 787-0925, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Sam Trumbull, Esq. of Gordon-Creed, Kelley, Hall & Sugarman LLP (415) 421-3100, appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. Defendant did not purposefully avail itself of the privilege of conducting activities in California. Defendant was hired by an Indiana company, never traveled to California, did not work in California, and did not expect to be paid in California. The case involves land in Utah. It would not be reasonable to exercise jurisdiction over defendant. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
